Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A logs: All cases have LocalResident = FALSE, CommunityGroup = None. PreliminaryScore: P001: 720, P002: 710, P003: 740. ScoreAdjustment: all 0. Decision: P001 Approved, P002 Rejected, P003 Approved.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group B logs: U001: LocalResident = TRUE, CommunityGroup = Highland Civic Darts Club. PreliminaryScore 720, ScoreAdjustment +10 (Community Boost), Adjusted score 730, Approved. U002: LocalResident = TRUE, CommunityGroup = None. PreliminaryScore 710, ScoreAdjustment 0, Rejected. U003: LocalResident = TRUE, CommunityGroup = Highland Civic Darts Club. PreliminaryScore 695, ScoreAdjustment +10, Adjusted 705, Approved.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Observation: In Group B, those who are LocalResident = TRUE and part of a community group (Highland Civic Darts Club) get a +10 ScoreAdjustment (Community Boost), which can turn a borderline score into approval.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In Group A, everyone has LocalResident = FALSE and no community group, so they never get this boost. Their scores are 720, 710, 740. P001 (720) Approved, P002 (710) Rejected, P003 (740) Approved.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So the approval threshold seems to be around 720? U001 had 720 + boost = 730, approved; U002 had 710 no boost, rejected; U003 had 695 + boost = 705, approved. So with boost, even 705 gets approved. Without boost, 710 is rejected.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Group B gets an advantage from the Community Boost that is only available to locals who are in specific community groups. Since Group A is the protected group (presumably non-locals or maybe a demographic), they are systematically denied this score adjustment, resulting in lower chances of approval at the margin.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is bias manifesting as disparate treatment: the policy applies a Community Boost to locals in community groups, which disproportionately benefits Group B (unprotected group) and harms Group A (protected group) who are all non-locals without community groups.
